title: Algorithmic Randomness: Schnorr, Prefix-free K & EML Depth

key_theorem.statement: The algorithmic randomness hierarchy maps onto EML depth: ML-random and Schnorr-random are EML-∞; Kurtz-random is EML-3 (envelope); computable-random is EML-2; periodic is EML-0. EML depth is the fine structure of algorithmic complexity: K(x) ≈ O(k·polylog n) for EML-k processes.

randomness_zoo.name: Martin-Löf random (ML-random)
randomness_zoo.definition: Passes all computable statistical tests; not in any ML-null set
randomness_zoo.eml_reason: No EML-finite generating function; digit function is incompressible

randomness_zoo.name: Schnorr random
randomness_zoo.definition: Passes all computable martingales with computable risk bounds
randomness_zoo.eml_reason: EML-∞ but 'uniformly' so; weaker than ML-random
examples: Some computable reals fail Schnorr; generic reals are Schnorr random

randomness_zoo.name: Kurtz random (weakly random)
randomness_zoo.definition: Not in any computably open null set
randomness_zoo.eml_reason: Weaker notion; computable approximation possible from outside → EML-3 envelope

randomness_zoo.name: Computable random
randomness_zoo.definition: Passes all computable statistical tests of 'simple' form
randomness_zoo.eml_reason: Tests are computable functions → EML-2 generators suffice to fool them

randomness_zoo.name: Periodic / EML-0
randomness_zoo.eml_reason: Digit function n↦d_n is EML-0 (period ≤ some constant)

coding_theorem.statement: m(x) = 2^{-K(x)} up to multiplicative constant, where m is the universal semimeasure
coding_theorem.eml_interpretation: m(x) = EML-1 atom in K(x): m = exp(-K(x)·ln 2); K = EML-∞ for random x means m→0

prefix_free_k.eml_k_correspondence: EML-k process generates strings with K(x_n) = O(k·polylog(n))

solovay_test.eml_solovay: The Solovay sum Σ 2^{-K(x_n)} is EML-1 per term; convergence = EML-∞ of x

connections.to_session_69: Session 69: Chaitin Ω, normal numbers. Session 80: deeper hierarchy (Schnorr, Solovay, prefix-free K)
connections.to_session_72: Approximation rate O(N^{-s}) ↔ K(x_N) ≈ s·log N: same information-theoretic content
